issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .btn:focus-visible

issuesFound.title: Mobile nav toggle function missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.open() and mobileNav.close() functions change the visibility of the navigation drawer but do not update the aria-expanded state on the trigger button. Screen reader users will not be informed of the menu's open/closed state.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2500: setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true);
Line 2525: setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu toggle functions missing aria-expanded update

Line 2700: setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true);
Line 2725: setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);

issuesFound.title: mobileNav toggle functions do not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation open, close, and toggle functions change the visibility of the drawer but do not set aria-expanded on the trigger button. Screen reader users cannot perceive the menu's open/closed state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2023: add `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true);` after `_open = true;`. Line 2029: add `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);` after `_open = false;`. Line 2035: update aria-expanded based on new state.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion toggle functions do not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The FAQ accordion open and close functions animate panel height but do not set aria-expanded on the trigger button. Screen reader users cannot know if answers are expanded.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3200: add `setAttr(item.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true);` after `item.open = true;`. Line 3218: add `setAttr(item.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);` after `item.open = false;`.

issuesFound.title: pricing toggle switch does not update aria-checked
issuesFound.explanation: The billing period toggle switch (month/year) changes visual state but does not set aria-checked on the switch element. Screen reader users cannot perceive the selected state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2805: add `setAttr(_toggleEl, ARIA.CHECKED, period === 'year');` inside _syncToggleState. Line 2812: ensure the same update occurs in _onToggleClick.

issuesFound.title: product filter updates not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: The applyFilters function updates the visible product grid but does not announce the result count via a live region. Screen reader users are not informed when filtered content changes.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2500: after `emit(_gridEl, 'filter:applied', ...);` add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.filterApplied(_productFilterState.activeCategory) || 'Filter applied.');`.

issuesFound.title: pricing period change not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: The setPeriod function updates price displays but does not announce the billing period change via a live region. Screen reader users are not informed when prices switch between monthly/annual.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2790: after `emit(document, 'pricing:periodchange', ...);` add `liveRegion.announce(period === 'year' ? STRINGS.announce.pricingAnnual : STRINGS.announce.pricingMonthly);`.

issuesFound.title: form validation errors not setting aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The inlineValidator _renderResult function marks fields invalid but does not set aria-invalid="true" on the input element. Screen readers cannot announce the invalid state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3405: in the invalid branch, add `field.setAttribute(ARIA.INVALID, 'true');`. In the valid branch, add `field.setAttribute(ARIA.INVALID, 'false');`.

issuesFound.title: mobile nav trigger does not have aria-expanded initialised on page load
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav init function does not set aria-expanded="false" on the trigger button when the drawer is closed on load. Screen reader users do not know the initial collapsed state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2070: after `_initialised = true;` add `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);`.

issuesFound.title: mobile nav drawer not hidden from accessibility tree when closed
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav close function hides the drawer visually but does not set aria-hidden="true". The drawer remains in the accessibility tree when closed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2029: after `removeClass(_drawer, CONFIG.navigation.openClass || CSS.open);` add `setAttr(_drawer, ARIA.HIDDEN, true);`. In open function, add `setAttr(_drawer, ARIA.HIDDEN, false);`.

issuesFound.title: mobile nav open/close not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav open and close functions do not announce state changes via a live region. Screen reader users are not informed when the menu opens or closes.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2023: after `emit(_trigger, 'mobilenav:open', ...);` add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.navOpen);`. Line 2029: after `emit(_trigger, 'mobilenav:close', ...);` add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.navClosed);`.

issuesFound.title: dropdown menu open/close not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu _openInstance and _closeInstance functions do not announce state changes via a live region. Screen reader users are not informed when dropdowns open or close.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2135: after `emit(inst.trigger, 'dropdown:open', ...);` add `liveRegion.announce('Dropdown opened');`. Line 2148: after `emit(inst.trigger, 'dropdown:close', ...);` add `liveRegion.announce('Dropdown closed');`.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion item open/close not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: The faqAccordion open and close functions do not announce state changes via a live region. Screen reader users are not informed when FAQ answers expand or collapse.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3200: after `emit(_containerEl, 'faq:open', ...);` add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.faqOpened(item.question));`. Line 3218: after `emit(_containerEl, 'faq:close', ...);` add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.faqClosed(item.question));`.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav toggle functions missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.open() and mobileNav.close() functions change the visibility of the navigation drawer but do not update aria-expanded on the trigger button. Screen‑reader users cannot perceive the menu’s open/closed state.
issuesFound.fix: In the mobileNav.open function, add setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');. In mobileNav.close, add setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu open/close functions missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu._openInstance and _closeInstance functions show/hide dropdown panels without updating aria-expanded on the associated trigger button. This breaks the ARIA disclosure pattern for screen‑reader users.
issuesFound.fix: In _openInstance, add setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');. In _closeInstance, add setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');.

issuesFound.title: pricing toggle switch missing aria-pressed/aria-checked update
issuesFound.explanation: The billing‑period toggle switch (role="switch") does not update aria-pressed or aria-checked when toggled between monthly and annual. Screen‑reader users cannot perceive the current toggle state.
issuesFound.fix: In _syncToggleState, add setAttr(_toggleEl, 'aria-checked', period === 'year' ? 'true' : 'false');.

issuesFound.title: productFilter applyFilters missing live‑region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: The productFilter.applyFilters function updates the visible card count and grid layout but does not announce the change via an aria‑live region. Screen‑reader users are not informed when filter results change.
issuesFound.fix: After _updateCountBadge, add liveRegion.announce(`${totalMatches} product${totalMatches !== 1 ? 's' : ''} now visible`);.

issuesFound.title: pricing setPeriod missing live‑region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing.setPeriod function changes displayed prices and billing period labels without announcing the switch to screen readers. Users who rely on live regions will not hear the update.
issuesFound.fix: After _updateSavingsMessage, add liveRegion.announce(`Prices switched to ${BILLING_PERIODS[period].label} billing`);.

issuesFound.title: inlineValidator does not set aria-invalid on invalid fields
issuesFound.explanation: The inlineValidator._renderResult function adds error classes and messages but does not set aria-invalid="true" on invalid input fields. Screen readers cannot programmatically detect validation errors.
issuesFound.fix: In the invalid branch of _renderResult, add setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true');. In the valid branch, add setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'false');.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger missing aria-expanded initialisation
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation trigger button does not have aria-expanded initialised to "false" on page load. Screen‑reader users cannot know the initial collapsed state of the menu.
issuesFound.fix: In mobileNav.init, after _trigger is assigned, add setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu triggers missing aria-expanded initialisation
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown trigger buttons are registered without an initial aria-expanded="false" attribute. Screen‑reader users are not informed that the dropdowns start in a collapsed state.
issuesFound.fix: In dropdownMenu.register, after setting aria-haspopup and aria-controls, add setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav drawer missing aria-hidden when hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation drawer is hidden by CSS classes but does not have aria-hidden="true" applied when closed. This leaves the hidden content in the accessibility tree, causing potential confusion.
issuesFound.fix: In mobileNav.close, add setAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden', 'true');. In mobileNav.open, add _drawer.removeAttribute('aria-hidden');.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu panels missing aria-hidden when hidden
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown panels are hidden via the hidden attribute but do not have aria-hidden="true" set. This can cause screen‑reader users to encounter hidden content in the accessibility tree.
issuesFound.fix: In _closeInstance, add setAttr(inst.panel, 'aria-hidden', 'true');. In _openInstance, add inst.panel.removeAttribute('aria-hidden');.

issuesFound.title: productFilter applyFilters missing live‑region announcement for count
issuesFound.explanation: When filters are applied, the updated product count is displayed visually but not announced to screen‑reader users via a live region. Users cannot perceive the change in result count.
issuesFound.fix: In applyFilters, after _updateCountBadge, add liveRegion.announce(`${totalMatches} product${totalMatches !== 1 ? 's' : ''} found`);.

issuesFound.title: pricing setPeriod missing live‑region announcement for period change
issuesFound.explanation: Switching billing periods updates price displays and savings messages without announcing the change via an aria‑live region. Screen‑reader users are not informed of the new billing period.
issuesFound.fix: In setPeriod, after _updateSavingsMessage, add liveRegion.announce(`Billing period changed to ${BILLING_PERIODS[period].label}`);.

fpTitles: dropdownMenu panels missing aria-hidden when hidden
productFilter applyFilters missing live‑region announcement for count

issuesFound.title: toggle function does not update aria-expanded on trigger element
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav.open` and `mobileNav.close` functions toggle the visibility of the navigation drawer but do not update the `aria-expanded` attribute on the trigger button. Screen reader users will not be notified whether the menu is expanded or collapsed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1041: `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');` (inside open), Line 1054: `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');` (inside close)

issuesFound.title: user action result not announced via aria-live region
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav.open` and `mobileNav.close` functions change the visibility of the navigation menu, but there is no corresponding write to a live region to announce the state change to screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1041: `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.navOpen);`, Line 1054: `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.navClosed);`

issuesFound.title: toggle button does not update aria-pressed

issuesFound.title: Negative letter-spacing impairs readability on body
issuesFound.explanation: The `body` rule also sets `letter-spacing: -0.05em`, tightening characters and reducing legibility for many readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line 141: `body { letter-spacing: normal; }`

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed with no alternative on a
issuesFound.explanation: The global `a` selector removes underlines (`text-decoration: none`) and no other visual differentiator (border, background, font‑weight) is provided for body‑text links, making it hard for users to identify links.
issuesFound.fix: Line 210: `a { text-decoration: underline; }`

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers‑reduced‑motion override on .btn
issuesFound.explanation: `.btn` defines a transition that includes `transform` (used on `:active`), creating movement. No `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)` rule disables this animation, violating users who request reduced motion.
issuesFound.fix: Line 420: `.btn { transition: background-color var(--transition-fast), border-color var(--transition-fast), color var(--transition-fast), box-shadow var(--transition-fast), transform var(--transition-fast); } @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .btn { transition: none; } }`

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers‑reduced‑motion override on .hero-image img
issuesFound.explanation: `.hero-image img` transitions `transform` and `box-shadow` on hover, creating spatial movement. No reduced‑motion override is provided.
issuesFound.fix: Line 640: `.hero-image img { transition: transform var(--transition-slower), box-shadow var(--transition-slower); } @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-image img, .hero-image img:hover { transition: none; transform: none; box-shadow: none; } }`

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers‑reduced‑motion override on .feature-card
issuesFound.explanation: `.feature-card` transitions `transform` (translateY) on hover, creating movement without a reduced‑motion safeguard.
issuesFound.fix: Line 610: `.feature-card { transition: border-color var(--transition-base), box-shadow var(--transition-base), transform var(--transition-base); } @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .feature-card:hover { transform: none; } }`

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers‑reduced‑motion override on .pricing-card
issuesFound.explanation: `.pricing-card` transitions `transform` (translateY) on hover, but no reduced‑motion media query disables it.
issuesFound.fix: Line 860: `.pricing-card { transition: box-shadow var(--transition-base), border-color var(--transition-base), transform var(--transition-base); } @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .pricing-card:hover { transform: none; } }`

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers‑reduced‑motion override on .product-card
issuesFound.explanation: `.product-card` transitions `transform` (translateY) on hover without a reduced‑motion override.
issuesFound.fix: Line 830: `.product-card { transition: box-shadow var(--transition-base), border-color var(--transition-base), transform var(--transition-base); } @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .product-card:hover { transform: none; } }`

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation trigger missing aria‑pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The hamburger button (`_trigger`) is used as a toggle but never receives `aria‑pressed`, so screen‑reader users cannot tell whether the menu is open.
issuesFound.fix:
After changing the open state, call `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-pressed', String(_open));`.

---

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu open function does not update aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: `dropdownMenu._openInstance()` shows the panel but never updates `aria‑expanded` on the trigger button, leaving the expanded state undisclosed to AT.
issuesFound.fix:
Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` when opening and set to `'false'` when closing.

---

issuesFound.title: Dropdown trigger button missing aria‑pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown trigger is a two‑state button (open/closed) but never receives `aria‑pressed`, so its state is invisible to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix:
In `_openInstance` set `aria‑pressed="true"` and in `_closeInstance` set `aria‑pressed="false"` on the trigger.

---

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion open function does not set aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: `faqAccordion.open()` expands an answer panel but never updates `aria‑expanded` on the associated trigger button, breaking the ARIA accordion pattern.
issuesFound.fix:
After opening, call `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` and set to `'false'` in the close routine.

---

issuesFound.title: FAQ trigger button missing aria‑pressed
issuesFound.explanation: FAQ question buttons act as toggles but are never given `aria‑pressed`, so their state cannot be conveyed.
issuesFound.fix:
When a question is opened set `aria‑pressed="true"` on the button; when closed set to `"false"`.

---

issuesFound.title: Search component does not update aria‑expanded on the input
issuesFound.explanation: `siteSearch._open()` shows the suggestion list but never sets `aria‑expanded` on the search `<input>`. Users of assistive tech cannot know that a list is now expanded.
issuesFound.fix:
In `_open()` add `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` and in `_close()` set it to `'false'`.

---

issuesFound.title: Pricing period toggle does not update aria‑pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The billing‑period buttons (`_periodBtns`) toggle between periods but never receive `aria‑pressed`, leaving their state ambiguous.
issuesFound.fix:
In `setPeriod()` after determining the active button, call `setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', String(isActive));` for each period button.

---

issuesFound.title: Inline validator does not set aria‑invalid on invalid fields
issuesFound.explanation: Validation results are shown visually, but the function never adds `aria‑invalid="true"` to fields that fail validation, violating WCAG 2.1 A.
issuesFound.fix:
In `_renderResult` when `result.valid` is false, call `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');` and remove it when valid.

---

issuesFound.title: Product filter updates count badge without live‑region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: After filtering, the product count badge text changes but no ARIA live region is updated, so screen‑reader users are unaware of the new count.
issuesFound.fix:
After setting the badge text, also update a hidden live‑region element (e.g., `liveRegion.announce(`${count} products shown`);`).

---

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation drawer hidden state not reflected with aria‑hidden
issuesFound.explanation: When the drawer is closed, it is only visually hidden; AT can still access its content because `aria‑hidden` is not applied.
issuesFound.fix:
In `close()` add `setAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden', 'true');` and in `open()` remove that attribute.

---

issuesFound.title: Dropdown panel hidden state not reflected with aria‑hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown panel is hidden via CSS but lacks `aria‑hidden="true"`, leaving its content in the accessibility tree.
issuesFound.fix:
In `_closeInstance` after hiding, call `setAttr(inst.panel, 'aria-hidden', 'true');` and remove it when opening.

---

issuesFound.title: FAQ answer panel hidden state not reflected with aria‑hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The answer `<dd>` is hidden visually but remains exposed to AT.
issuesFound.fix:
In `close()` add `setAttr(item.panel, 'aria-hidden', 'true');` and remove it in `open()`.

---

issuesFound.title: Search suggestion list hidden state not reflected with aria‑hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The listbox is hidden visually but not marked with `aria-hidden`, so AT may still read it.
issuesFound.fix:
In `hide()` also call `setAttr(_listEl, 'aria-hidden', 'true');` and remove it in `show()`.

---

issuesFound.title: User actions (opening mobile nav, dropdowns, FAQ, pricing toggle) are not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: These interactive actions change visible UI but no `liveRegion.announce` call follows, leaving AT users unaware of the change.
issuesFound.fix: After each state change, add an appropriate announcement, e.g., `liveRegion.announce('Main navigation opened');`, `liveRegion.announce('Filter options expanded');`, `liveRegion.announce('Annual billing selected');`, etc.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown trigger does not update aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The button that opens a dropdown menu never receives an aria‑expanded state change when the panel is shown or hidden, so assistive technologies cannot convey the menu’s open/closed status.
issuesFound.fix: In _openInstance add setAttr(trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true) and in _closeInstance add setAttr(trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false).

issuesFound.title: Dropdown trigger missing aria‑pressed state
issuesFound.explanation: The hamburger button toggles the mobile navigation drawer but never updates aria‑pressed, which is required for a two‑state toggle button.
issuesFound.fix: After toggle() calls open() or close() add setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.PRESSED, String(_open)) or setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.PRESSED, 'false').

issuesFound.title: Dropdown panel hidden without aria‑hidden
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown panel is closed the code sets hidden but does not set aria‑hidden="true", leaving the element in the accessibility tree.
issuesFound.fix: In _closeInstance add setAttr(panel, ARIA.HIDDEN, 'true') before adding the hidden attribute, and remove it in _openInstance.

issuesFound.title: FAQ answer panel hidden without aria‑hidden
issuesFound.explanation: Closing an FAQ item only adds hidden to the panel; aria‑hidden is not set, so the hidden content remains perceivable to assistive tech.
issuesFound.fix: In close add setAttr(item.panel, ARIA.HIDDEN, 'true') and remove it in open.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation drawer hidden without aria‑hidden
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile nav drawer is closed the code removes the CSS class but never sets aria‑hidden="true" on the drawer element, leaving it in the accessibility tree.
issuesFound.fix: In close add setAttr(_drawer, ARIA.HIDDEN, 'true') and in open remove that attribute.

issuesFound.title: Product‑filter UI updates are not announced via a live region
issuesFound.explanation: Changing filters updates the visible product count and empty‑state message, but no aria‑live region is updated, so users of assistive technology receive no feedback about the result of their filter action.
issuesFound.fix: After the UI update, set statusEl.textContent with the new count and add setAttr(statusEl, 'aria‑live', 'polite') or use liveRegion.announce to convey the change.

issuesFound.title: Pricing period change does not announce the new price to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: Switching between monthly/annual pricing updates the numbers on the cards but does not write to any live region, leaving screen‑reader users unaware of the price change.
issuesFound.fix: After updating each plan’s price, call liveRegion.announce with a concise message such as “Annual pricing selected – $99 per year”.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown trigger lacks initial aria‑expanded state on page load
issuesFound.explanation: The trigger button is created without an aria‑expanded attribute, so its default state is ambiguous to assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix: In register add setAttr(trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false') when the panel is initially hidden.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion trigger missing initial aria‑expanded when closed
issuesFound.explanation: Closed FAQ items lack aria‑expanded="false", making their state unclear on load.
issuesFound.fix: In init ensure every trigger receives setAttr(trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false') unless it is opened by default.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown trigger missing aria‑controls reference to its panel (when panel has an id)
issuesFound.explanation: The code adds aria‑controls only if the panel has an id, but many panels lack an id, leaving the relationship undisclosed.
issuesFound.fix: Ensure each panel receives a generated id (e.g., srUtils.ensureId(panel, 'dropdown-panel')) and always set aria‑controls on the trigger.

issuesFound.title: Sticky header adds/removes CSS classes without updating aria‑expanded or aria‑hidden on the header element for assistive tech
issuesFound.explanation: The header is hidden on scroll‑down by adding a CSS class, but no aria‑hidden attribute is toggled, so screen‑reader users may still navigate to the hidden header.
issuesFound.fix: When the header is hidden, call setAttr(_header, ARIA.HIDDEN, 'true'); when shown, remove that attribute.

issuesFound.title: Toggle function does not update aria‑expanded (HIGH)
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown menu’s internal toggle functions (`_openInstance` / `_closeInstance`) change visibility of the panel but never set or update `aria‑expanded` on the trigger button, leaving screen‑reader users unaware of the menu state.
issuesFound.fix:
In both `_openInstance` and `_closeInstance` add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true')` when opening and `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false')` when closing.

---

issuesFound.title: Toggle button does not update aria‑pressed (HIGH)
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing period buttons (`_onPeriodBtnClick`) act as a two‑state toggle (monthly / yearly) but never set `aria‑pressed` (or `aria‑checked`) to reflect the current selection.
issuesFound.fix:
After changing the period, call `setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', btnPeriod === _pricingState.period ? 'true' : 'false')` for each period button.

---

issuesFound.title: Dynamic content update not announced via live region (MEDIUM)
issuesFound.explanation: When a FAQ item is opened, the visible change (panel becomes visible) is not announced to screen readers; no live‑region update occurs.
issuesFound.fix:
After opening, call `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.faqOpened(item.question) || 'Answer expanded')`; similarly announce on close.

---

issuesFound.title: Form validation errors not reflected in aria‑invalid (HIGH)
issuesFound.explanation: The inline validation system marks fields as invalid visually but never sets `aria-invalid="true"` (or removes it) on the input, breaking the native ARIA mapping of required/invalid state.
issuesFound.fix:
In `_renderResult`, when `!result.valid` call `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true')`; when valid, call `removeAttr(field, 'aria-invalid')`.

---

issuesFound.title: Toggle trigger missing initial aria‑expanded (MEDIUM)
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown is registered, the trigger button receives `aria-controls` but never receives an initial `aria-expanded="false"` attribute, so its collapsed state is not exposed on page load.
issuesFound.fix:
In `register`, after setting `aria-controls`, also call `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false')`.

---

issuesFound.title: Dynamically hidden element not updated with aria‑hidden (MEDIUM)
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown close routine sets `hidden` on the panel but does not set `aria-hidden="true"`, so the element remains in the accessibility tree while invisible.
issuesFound.fix:
In `_closeInstance`, after `inst.panel.setAttribute('hidden', '')`, also call `setAttr(inst.panel, 'aria-hidden', 'true')`. Conversely, `_openInstance` should remove `aria-hidden`.

---

issuesFound.title: User action not announced via live region (MEDIUM)
issuesFound.explanation: Changing the billing period updates prices and UI but does not announce the change to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix:
After `setPeriod(period)`, add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.pricingPeriodChanged || 'Billing period changed to ' + period)`.

---

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.open and mobileNav.close functions toggle the visibility of the navigation drawer but do not update the aria-expanded state on the trigger button. Screen reader users will not be informed whether the menu is open or closed when interacting with the toggle.

Line 2955: setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');
  Line 2975: setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');

issuesFound.title: mobileNav overlay not removed from accessibility tree

Line 2955: _overlay.removeAttribute('aria-hidden');
  Line 2970: _overlay.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'true');

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu trigger does not update aria-expanded

issuesFound.explanation: The logo bar label uses colour-neutral-400 (#94a3b8) on colour-neutral-50 (#f8fafc) background, resulting in 2.8:1 contrast that fails WCAG AA requirements for text readability.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 745:

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast: foreground #aaaaaa / background #ffffff ratio 2.8:1 on .input-hint
issuesFound.explanation: Input hints use colour-text-secondary (#aaaaaa) on white background, providing insufficient 2.8:1 contrast that fails WCAG AA standards and makes helper text difficult to read for users with visual impairments.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast: foreground #94a3b8 / background #555555 ratio 3.0:1 on footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a
issuesFound.explanation: Footer navigation links use colour-neutral-400 (#94a3b8) on colour-neutral-900 (#555555) background, resulting in 3.0:1 contrast that fails the 4.5:1 requirement for normal text under WCAG AA.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast: foreground #94a3b8 / background #555555 ratio 3.0:1 on .footer-brand > p
issuesFound.explanation: Footer brand paragraphs use colour-neutral-400 (#94a3b8) on the dark footer background (#555555), providing only 3.0:1 contrast which is below the 4.5:1 required for WCAG AA compliance.

Line 1397:

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast: foreground #64748b / background #555555 ratio 1.5:1 on .footer-brand address
issuesFound.explanation: Footer address text uses colour-neutral-500 (#64748b) on the dark footer background (#555555), resulting in 1.5:1 contrast that severely fails WCAG AA requirements and makes contact information unreadable for many users.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast: foreground #64748b / background #555555 ratio 1.5:1 on .footer-bottom > p

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed with no alternative on #nav-menu > li > a
issuesFound.explanation: Primary navigation links inherit text-decoration: none from the global anchor rule and provide no non-color visual differentiator (such as underline, border-bottom, or font-weight change) at rest, relying solely on color to distinguish them from surrounding text.

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed with no alternative on nav[aria-label="Account navigation"] a
issuesFound.explanation: Account navigation links remove underlines without providing an alternative visual differentiator at rest, forcing users to rely on color alone to identify links, which fails WCAG 1.4.1 Use of Color.

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed with no alternative on footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a
issuesFound.explanation: Footer navigation links remove underlines and rely solely on color (neutral-400) to indicate they are links, providing no alternative visual indicator for users who cannot perceive color differences.

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed with no alternative on .footer-brand address a
issuesFound.explanation: Address links in the footer brand section remove underlines without providing a non-color visual differentiator at rest, making them indistinguishable from plain text for colorblind users.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1366: 
.footer-brand address a { color: var(--colour-neutral-400); text-decoration: underline; transition: color var(--transition-fast); display: inline; }

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed with no alternative on .footer-bottom > p a
issuesFound.explanation: Links in the footer bottom section remove underlines and rely only on color to indicate they are clickable, failing to provide a non-color visual differentiator required by WCAG 1.4.1.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1413: 
.footer-bottom > p a { color: var(--colour-neutral-400); text-decoration: underline; transition: color var(--transition-fast); }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .focus-ring-inset:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: This utility modifier changes the outline offset but maintains outline: none from the parent rule, resulting in no visible focus indicator when the class is applied.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .focus-ring-brand:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The rule sets outline-color but outline remains none from the global :focus-visible rule, producing no visible focus ring. Setting only outline-color without an outline style has no visual effect.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .header-inner > a:first-child:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The logo link removes the focus outline without providing a visible alternative, making it impossible for keyboard users to see when the link has focus.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .nav-toggle:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation toggle button removes the focus outline with no replacement, preventing keyboard users from identifying when the control is focused.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on [id$="-submenu"] li a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown menu links remove the focus outline using outline: none and negative outline-offset without providing a visible alternative focus indicator.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on #search-input:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The search input overrides the visible box-shadow from the :focus rule with outline: none and no replacement, removing the focus indicator for keyboard users.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on #site-search button[type="submit"]:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The search submit button removes the focus outline without providing a visible alternative, leaving no indicator for keyboard users.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on nav[aria-label="Account navigation"] a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Account navigation links remove the focus outline with no replacement, preventing keyboard users from identifying the focused link.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .feature-card a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Feature card links remove the focus outline without providing a visible alternative, making keyboard navigation invisible.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .pricing-toggle button:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Pricing toggle buttons remove the focus outline without providing a visible alternative, preventing keyboard users from seeing which button is focused.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .table-wrapper:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The scrollable table wrapper removes the focus outline with no replacement, making it impossible for keyboard users to see when the region has focus.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .faq-item dt button:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: FAQ accordion buttons remove the focus outline using outline: none with only a negative offset, providing no visible focus indicator.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .form-group input:focus-visible, .form-group select:focus-visible, .form-group textarea:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Form inputs override the visible box-shadow from the :focus rule with outline: none and no replacement, removing focus indicators for keyboard users.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .form-group input[type="checkbox"]:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Checkbox inputs remove the focus outline with no visible replacement, preventing keyboard users from seeing when the checkbox is focused.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .footer-brand a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Footer brand links remove the focus outline without providing a visible alternative.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .social-links a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Social media links remove the focus outline without providing a visible alternative.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement in forced-colors mode
issuesFound.explanation: The @media (forced-colors: active) block removes the focus outline with outline: none and only sets outline-offset, providing no visible focus indicator in high-contrast mode.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: .btn-sm
issuesFound.explanation: The small button variant sets min-height: 20px, which is below the 24px minimum touch target size required by WCAG 2.5.8, making it difficult for users with motor impairments to activate.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: .btn-icon
issuesFound.explanation: The icon button sets min-width: 20px and min-height: 20px, creating a touch target smaller than the 24px minimum, which is difficult for users with motor impairments to activate accurately.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: input[type="checkbox"]
issuesFound.explanation: Form checkboxes are sized at 20px × 20px, which is below the 24px minimum touch target size, making them difficult for users with motor impairments to select accurately.

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility removes content from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class uses position: static, width: auto, height: auto, and overflow: visible, which fails to visually hide content while keeping it in the accessibility tree. Screen reader text will be visible on screen, and assistive technology may not recognize it as hidden.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .btn:active
issuesFound.explanation: The button active state uses transform: translateY(1px) for spatial movement, but there is no @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) override to disable this animation for users who prefer reduced motion.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .hero-image img
issuesFound.explanation: The hero image uses transition on transform and a hover state with perspective rotation, but the @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block at line 1244 is empty and does not disable these spatial animations.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .feature-card a:hover::after
issuesFound.explanation: The feature card link arrow uses transform: translateX(3px) on hover for spatial movement, but the existing @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block only covers .feature-card:hover, not the ::after pseudo-element.

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: The body element sets word-spacing: -0.2em, which reduces the space between words below normal. This can make text difficult to read for users with dyslexia or cognitive disabilities, and may cause words to overlap at certain zoom levels.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast: --colour-text-secondary on white background
issuesFound.explanation: The colour #aaaaaa (--colour-text-secondary) on white (#ffffff) produces a contrast ratio of approximately 2.2:1, which fails the WCAG 4.5:1 minimum for normal text. This affects secondary text throughout the interface.
issuesFound.fix: --colour-text-secondary: #767676;

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast: placeholder text (--colour-neutral-400)
issuesFound.explanation: The placeholder colour #94a3b8 (--colour-neutral-400) on white produces a contrast ratio of approximately 2.4:1, failing the 4.5:1 minimum required for text input placeholders.
issuesFound.fix: --colour-neutral-400: #6b7280;

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast: --colour-error on white background
issuesFound.explanation: The error colour #ee9999 on white produces a contrast ratio of approximately 2.1:1, failing the 4.5:1 minimum required for text. This makes required field indicators difficult to see for users with low vision.
issuesFound.fix: --colour-error: #d32f2f;

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast: .stat-label on gradient background
issuesFound.explanation: The stat label uses rgba(255,255,255,0.3) (30% white) on a gradient background ranging from #5588bb to #bb88ee, producing a contrast ratio of approximately 2.5:1 or less, failing the 4.5:1 minimum for small text.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast: .step-number white text on brand secondary
issuesFound.explanation: The step number uses white text on a gradient background including #bb88ee (--colour-brand-secondary), producing a contrast ratio of approximately 1.6:1, which fails the 4.5:1 minimum required for text.

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed with no alternative
issuesFound.explanation: The global a selector removes text-decoration: none from all links without providing a non-colour visual differentiator (such as underline on hover, border-bottom, or font-weight change) for all link instances. Many links (navigation, social) rely solely on colour to distinguish them from surrounding text.

fpTitles: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .faq-item dt button:focus-visible
Touch target below minimum size: input[type="checkbox"]
Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .hero-image img
Insufficient colour contrast: --colour-text-secondary on white background
Insufficient colour contrast: placeholder text (--colour-neutral-400)

issuesFound.title: Mobile nav trigger button missing aria-controls attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The trigger button controls the navigation drawer element, but it lacks an aria-controls attribute pointing to the drawer's id. This prevents screen reader users from understanding the relationship between the button and the controlled region.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1 (in mobileNav.init after _drawer assignment): Add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-controls', srUtils.ensureId(_drawer, 'mobile-nav'));`

issuesFound.title: Mobile nav drawer element missing aria-hidden when closed
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation drawer is closed, it is visually hidden but remains in the accessibility tree because the code does not set aria-hidden="true" on the drawer element. Screen reader users may still perceive the hidden content.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1 (in mobileNav.close): Add `setAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden', 'true');` Line 2 (in mobileNav.open): Add `removeAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden');` or `setAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: Mobile nav open function missing focus trap activation
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation drawer opens, keyboard focus is not trapped within the drawer. The _trap variable is declared but never initialized or activated, allowing keyboard users to tab outside the modal drawer into the background content.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1 (in mobileNav.open after _lockScroll): Add `_trap = createFocusTrap(_drawer, { returnFocus: _trigger }); _trap.activate();` Line 2 (in mobileNav.close): Add `if (_trap) { _trap.deactivate(); _trap = null; }`

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu trigger button missing aria-expanded state management
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown menu toggle functions show and hide the panel by manipulating the hidden attribute and CSS classes, but they do not set aria-expanded on the trigger button to indicate the panel's visibility state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1 (in dropdownMenu._openInstance): Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` Line 2 (in dropdownMenu._closeInstance): Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` Line 3 (in dropdownMenu.register): Add `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` to initialize.

issuesFound.title: Billing period switch element missing aria-checked attribute
issuesFound.explanation: When the billing toggle is not a native button, it is given role="switch" but the aria-checked attribute is never set to reflect whether annual or monthly billing is selected. Screen readers cannot convey the switch state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1 (in pricing._syncToggleState): Add `if (_toggleEl && _toggleEl.getAttribute('role') === 'switch') { setAttr(_toggleEl, 'aria-checked', String(_pricingState.period === 'year')); }` Line 2 (in pricing.init after setting role): Add `setAttr(_toggleEl, 'aria-checked', String(_pricingState.period === 'year'));`

issuesFound.title: Pricing period change missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: When the billing period is switched (e.g., monthly to annual), the price values update visually across all plan cards, but screen reader users receive no notification that prices have changed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1 (in pricing.setPeriod after updating plan cards): Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.pricingPeriodChanged || 'Prices updated to ${period} billing');`

issuesFound.title: Wizard progress bar element missing role="progressbar"
issuesFound.explanation: The wizard progress bar element has aria-valuemin, aria-valuemax, aria-valuenow, and aria-valuetext attributes set, but lacks role="progressbar". Screen readers do not recognize it as a progress indicator.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1 (in multiStepWizard.init): Add `if (_progressBar && !_progressBar.getAttribute('role')) { setAttr(_progressBar, 'role', 'progressbar'); }`

fpTitles: Wizard progress bar element missing role="progressbar"

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.close() does not set aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The function closes the mobile navigation drawer but fails to set aria-expanded="false" on the trigger button, leaving screen reader users unaware that the panel is collapsed.
issuesFound.fix: Line (in close() after removeClass(_drawer,...)): setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.init() missing aria-controls on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The initialization establishes the relationship between the toggle button and the drawer but does not set aria-controls on the trigger to programmatically associate it with the drawer element.
issuesFound.fix: Line (in init() after _drawer assignment): setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.CONTROLS, srUtils.ensureId(_drawer, 'nav-drawer'));

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.init() missing aria-expanded initialization
issuesFound.explanation: The trigger button starts with the drawer hidden on page load, but the code does not initialize aria-expanded="false", leaving assistive technologies unaware of the initial state.
issuesFound.fix: Line (in init() after _trigger assignment): setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance() does not set aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The function reveals a dropdown panel but fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button, preventing screen reader users from knowing the dropdown is open.
issuesFound.fix: Line (in _openInstance() after inst.panel.removeAttribute('hidden')): setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true);

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._closeInstance() does not set aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The function hides the dropdown panel but fails to set aria-expanded="false" on the trigger button, leaving screen reader users unaware the dropdown is closed.
issuesFound.fix: Line (in _closeInstance() after removeClass(inst.panel,...)): setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu.register() missing aria-expanded initialization
issuesFound.explanation: When registering a new dropdown, the panel is hidden with setAttribute('hidden', '') but the trigger's aria-expanded is not initialized to "false" to reflect the closed state on page load.
issuesFound.fix: Line (in register() after panel.setAttribute('hidden', '')): setAttr(trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);

issuesFound.title: pricing.setPeriod() does not update aria-pressed on period buttons
issuesFound.explanation: The billing period toggle buttons cycle between active/inactive states, but the code does not set aria-pressed="true" on the active button or aria-pressed="false" on inactive buttons to indicate the toggle state to assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Line (in _syncToggleState() inside _periodBtns.forEach): setAttr(btn, ARIA.PRESSED, String(isActive));

issuesFound.title: pricing.init() missing aria-pressed initialization
issuesFound.explanation: The active billing period button is determined on initialization, but aria-pressed is not set to reflect which button is initially pressed.
issuesFound.fix: Line (in init() after setPeriod(initPeriod, false)): Add loop: _periodBtns.forEach(btn => { const isActive = btn.getAttribute('data-period') === initPeriod; setAttr(btn, ARIA.PRESSED, String(isActive)); });

issuesFound.title: siteSearch._open() does not set aria-expanded on combobox
issuesFound.explanation: The search input acts as a combobox that opens a suggestion list, but the function does not set aria-expanded="true" to indicate the popup is visible.
issuesFound.fix: Line (in _open() after _searchState.open = true): setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.EXPANDED, true);

issuesFound.title: siteSearch._close() does not set aria-expanded on combobox
issuesFound.explanation: The function closes the suggestion list but does not set aria-expanded="false" to indicate the popup is hidden.
issuesFound.fix: Line (in _close() after _searchState.open = false): setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);

issuesFound.title: siteSearch.init() missing aria-expanded initialization
issuesFound.explanation: The combobox input starts with the suggestion list hidden, but aria-expanded is not initialized to "false" on page load.
issuesFound.fix: Line (in init() after suggestionListRenderer.mount(_inputEl)): setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid() does not set aria-invalid="true"
issuesFound.explanation: The function links an error message to a field via aria-describedby but fails to set aria-invalid="true" on the invalid field, so screen readers do not announce the field as invalid.
issuesFound.fix: Line (in markInvalid() after addDescribedBy(field, errorEl)): setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'true');

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid() does not clear aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The function removes the error description association but does not set aria-invalid="false" or remove the aria-invalid attribute, leaving the field marked as invalid for assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Line (in markValid() after the if block handling errorEl): setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'false');

issuesFound.title: testimonialsCarousel.play() does not set aria-pressed on play button
issuesFound.explanation: The play/pause toggle button starts auto-play, but the code does not set aria-pressed="true" to indicate the "playing" state is active.
issuesFound.fix: Line (in play() after _playing = true): setAttr(_playBtn, ARIA.PRESSED, 'true');

issuesFound.title: testimonialsCarousel.pause() does not set aria-pressed on play button
issuesFound.explanation: The function pauses the carousel but does not set aria-pressed="false" on the toggle button to indicate the paused state.
issuesFound.fix: Line (in pause() after _playing = false): setAttr(_playBtn, ARIA.PRESSED, 'false');

issuesFound.title: productFilter.applyFilters() missing live region announcement for result count
issuesFound.explanation: After filtering products, the visible result count updates but no aria-live announcement is made to inform screen reader users how many products are now displayed.
issuesFound.fix: Line (in applyFilters() inside filterAnimation.run callback, after _updateCountBadge): liveRegion.announce(`${totalMatches} product${totalMatches !== 1 ? 's' : ''} found`);

issuesFound.title: pricing.setPeriod() missing live region announcement for billing change
issuesFound.explanation: When the billing period changes (e.g., monthly to annual), prices update visually but the change is not announced to screen reader users via a live region.
issuesFound.fix: Line (in setPeriod() after _updateSavingsMessage(period)): liveRegion.announce(`Prices switched to ${BILLING_PERIODS[period].label.toLowerCase()} billing`);

issuesFound.title: scrollToTop._onClick() missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: Clicking the scroll-to-top button scrolls the page to the top but does not announce the action completion to screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix: Line (in _onClick() after scrollToY(0)): liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.scrolledToTop || 'Scrolled to top of page');

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open() missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: Opening the mobile navigation drawer changes the page context significantly, but no announcement is made to inform screen reader users that the navigation menu is now open.
issuesFound.fix: Line (in open() after emit()): liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.navOpen || 'Navigation menu opened');

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.close() missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: Closing the mobile navigation drawer removes content from view, but no announcement informs screen reader users that the menu is now closed.
issuesFound.fix: Line (in close() after emit()): liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.navClosed || 'Navigation menu closed');

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance() missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: Opening a dropdown menu reveals new content, but the change is not announced to screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix: Line (in _openInstance() after emit()): liveRegion.announce(`${inst.trigger.textContent} submenu opened`);

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._closeInstance() missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: Closing a dropdown menu hides content, but no announcement informs screen reader users that the menu is closed.
issuesFound.fix: Line (in _closeInstance() after emit()): liveRegion.announce(`${inst.trigger.textContent} submenu closed`);

issuesFound.title: siteSearch._selectSuggestion() missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: When a user selects a search suggestion, the page navigates or updates, but no announcement confirms the selection action to screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix: Line (in _selectSuggestion() before setTimeout): liveRegion.announce(`Selected ${item.label}`);

issuesFound.title: mobileNav missing focus trap implementation
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation drawer acts as a modal but does not trap keyboard focus; the _trap variable is declared but never instantiated with createFocusTrap(), allowing users to tab out of the drawer while it is open.
issuesFound.fix: Line (in init() after _drawer assignment): _trap = createFocusTrap(_drawer, { returnFocus: true }); Line (in open()): _trap.activate(); Line (in close()): _trap.deactivate();

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.close() missing focus return to trigger
issuesFound.explanation: When the drawer closes, focus is not returned to the trigger button that opened it, leaving keyboard users stranded at the bottom of the page or in an unpredictable location.
issuesFound.fix: Line (in close() at end of function, before emit): if (_trigger) _trigger.focus();

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu triggers missing aria-expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown panels are shown and hidden, but the trigger button's aria-expanded state is not synchronized with the panel visibility. The attribute is initialized in register() but never updated when the panel opens or closes.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1952: setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true);
  Line 1967: setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion triggers missing aria-expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: FAQ answer panels are expanded and collapsed using animateHeight, but the trigger button's aria-expanded attribute is never set to "true" when opened or "false" when closed. The initial state is set in init() but subsequent toggles do not update the attribute.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 3720: setAttr(item.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true);
  Line 3740: setAttr(item.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);

issuesFound.title: Pricing period buttons missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The billing period buttons (monthly/quarterly/annual) function as toggle buttons but do not have aria-pressed attributes to indicate which period is currently selected. Screen readers cannot convey the active state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3277: setAttr(btn, ARIA.PRESSED, String(isActive));

issuesFound.title: Form validation missing aria-invalid attribute
issuesFound.explanation: When fields are validated, the code adds/removes error classes and updates aria-describedby, but never sets aria-invalid="true" on invalid fields or aria-invalid="false" on valid fields. Screen readers cannot convey the validation state.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 4162: setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, true);

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation focus trap not activated
